name: WooCommerce
description: AI-native commerce operations for any WooCommerce store. Semantic tools for products, variations, inventory, orders, refunds, customers, categories, and reports — plus documented multi-step recipes (inventory optimisation, SEO refresh, upsell/cross-sell, abandoned-order triage, revenue analysis).
instructions:
This connector talks to the WooCommerce REST API v3 (`/wp-json/wc/v3`). Works against any WordPress + WooCommerce site. HTTPS required.

## Setup

1. **`WOOCOMMERCE_URL`** — site root (e.g. `https://shop.example.com`). Do NOT include `/wp-json`; the adapter appends `/wp-json/wc/v3`.
2. **`WOOCOMMERCE_CONSUMER_KEY`** + **`WOOCOMMERCE_CONSUMER_SECRET`** — generate in WP admin → WooCommerce → Settings → Advanced → REST API → Add key. Use **Read/Write** for full functionality.

HTTPS is mandatory: on HTTP, WC requires OAuth1 instead of Basic Auth (the connector only does Basic Auth).

## Pagination

List endpoints default to `per_page=10`, max `100`. Pass explicit `per_page` + iterate `page` for full catalog scans. Total counts are in `X-WP-Total` / `X-WP-TotalPages` headers (not returned in the JSON body).

## Bulk writes

For ≥ 3 product updates use `woocommerce_batch_update_products` (one call, up to 100 entries). Don't fan out single `update_product` calls.

## SEO meta (Yoast / Rank Math) on products

Meta lives in the `meta_data` array on the product. Set it via `woocommerce_update_product`:

```json
"meta_data": [
  { "key": "_yoast_wpseo_metadesc", "value": "..." },
  { "key": "_yoast_wpseo_focuskw", "value": "..." }
]
```

Rank Math: `rank_math_description`, `rank_math_focus_keyword`, `rank_math_title`. Full reference: `wordpress_list_seo_meta_keys` (sibling WordPress connector).

## Multi-step workflows

For inventory optimisation, product SEO, upsell/cross-sell, low-conversion analysis, abandoned-order triage, or KPI snapshots → call the dedicated **`woocommerce_skill_*`** tool. It returns the step-by-step playbook on demand. Don't preload that knowledge here.

## Enum reference cards

Valid values for order status / product type / stock status / report period / payment methods → call **`woocommerce_list_*_options`**.

## Permissions cheat-sheet

- All list / get / reports: Read-only key.
- Product create/update/delete, order update, refund, batch: Read/Write key.
- Customer create / coupon create: Read/Write key.

tools.name: woocommerce_search_products
tools.description: Search/list products with rich filters — keyword (`search`), SKU, status, type (simple/variable/grouped/external), stock_status, on_sale, price range, category, tag, featured. Paginate with `page` + `per_page` (max 100).

status.description: any | draft | pending | private | publish. Default: publish.

orderby.description: date | id | include | title | slug | price | popularity | rating | menu_order.

tools.name: woocommerce_get_product
tools.description: Get a single product by id, including all fields: name, slug, description, short_description, sku, price/regular_price/sale_price, stock_quantity, stock_status, categories, tags, images, attributes, variations array (for variable products), upsell_ids, cross_sell_ids, meta_data.

tools.name: woocommerce_create_product
tools.description: Create a product. Minimal required: `name`. For variable products set `type=variable` and add variations via `woocommerce_create_product_variation` after creation. Pass `meta_data` array for Yoast / Rank Math SEO keys.

type.description: simple | grouped | external | variable. Default: simple.

dimensions.description: { length, width, height } — strings (cm or per store unit).

images.description: Array of { id } (existing media) or { src } (URL — WC will sideload). First image = featured.

attributes.description: Product attributes — see WC REST docs.

upsell_ids.description: Product IDs to show as upsells.

cross_sell_ids.description: Product IDs to show as cross-sells.

meta_data.description: Array of { key, value } — for Yoast/Rank Math SEO keys.

tools.name: woocommerce_update_product
tools.description: Partial-update a product by id. Only fields you pass are touched. Use for single-product edits; for bulk use `woocommerce_batch_update_products`.

dimensions.description: { length, width, height } — strings (cm or per store unit).

tools.name: woocommerce_delete_product
tools.description: Move a product to trash, or pass `force=true` to delete permanently (cannot be undone).

force.description: true = permanent delete; false (default) = move to trash.

tools.name: woocommerce_batch_update_products
tools.description: Bulk create / update / delete products in a single call. WooCommerce allows up to 100 entries per array. Best primitive for inventory bumps, SEO refresh waves, or category re-tags.

create.description: Array of product objects to create.

update.description: Array of product objects — each MUST include an `id` field.

delete.description: Array of product IDs to delete.

tools.name: woocommerce_list_product_variations
tools.description: List variations for a variable product. Returns id, sku, price, stock_quantity, attributes per variation.

tools.name: woocommerce_update_product_variation
tools.description: Update a single variation of a variable product — price, SKU, stock, attributes.

tools.name: woocommerce_list_product_categories
tools.description: List product categories. Returns id, name, slug, parent, count.

tools.name: woocommerce_create_product_category
tools.description: Create a product category.

image.description: { id } or { src } for the category image.

tools.name: woocommerce_list_product_tags
tools.description: List product tags.

tools.name: woocommerce_list_product_attributes
tools.description: List the global attribute definitions configured for the store (e.g. Size, Colour). Needed when building variable products.

tools.name: woocommerce_find_low_stock
tools.description: Find products that are out of stock OR have `manage_stock=true` with `stock_quantity` at/below `threshold`. Pages through the catalog using `per_page` (max 100) and the supplied `page`. Returns the raw `/products` page filtered by stock status — combine with the agent's own threshold check on `stock_quantity` if needed. For the simple case, set `stock_status=outofstock` or `stock_status=onbackorder`.

tools.name: woocommerce_update_stock
tools.description: Convenience wrapper over `update_product`: enables stock management and sets quantity + status in one call. Use for single-product stock bumps; for bulk use `batch_update_products`.

tools.name: woocommerce_list_orders
tools.description: List orders with status, date, customer, and product filters. For abandoned-order triage use status=pending,on-hold,failed with a recent `after`.

status.description: Comma-separated: any | pending | processing | on-hold | completed | cancelled | refunded | failed | trash.

product.description: Filter to orders that include this product id.

tools.name: woocommerce_get_order
tools.description: Get a single order by id — full payload incl. line_items, billing, shipping, customer_id, totals, payment, refunds.

tools.name: woocommerce_update_order_status
tools.description: Set an order's status (e.g. processing → completed, on-hold → cancelled). Triggers WooCommerce's email + stock side-effects associated with the new status.

status.description: pending | processing | on-hold | completed | cancelled | refunded | failed.

tools.name: woocommerce_add_order_note
tools.description: Add a note to an order. Customer-visible notes trigger an email; private notes are admin-only (audit trail).

customer_note.description: true = customer-visible + emailed; false (default) = private admin note.

tools.name: woocommerce_list_refunds
tools.description: List refunds attached to an order.

tools.name: woocommerce_create_refund
tools.description: Create a refund against an order. Pass an `amount` for a partial refund, or `api_refund=true` to also trigger refund via the payment gateway (Stripe, PayPal, etc.) when supported. Pass `line_items` to refund specific line items with their quantities.

amount.description: Decimal as string. Omit for full refund.

api_refund.description: When true (WC default), also refund via the payment gateway API (Stripe, PayPal, ...) where supported. Pass false to record the refund in WC only — useful for manual / bank-transfer flows.

line_items.description: Array of { id, quantity, refund_total, refund_tax }.

tools.name: woocommerce_list_customers
tools.description: List customers. Filter by email, role, or registration date.

tools.name: woocommerce_get_customer
tools.description: Get a customer by id (includes billing/shipping addresses, order count, total spent, last_order info).

tools.name: woocommerce_sales_report
tools.description: Sales report for a date window or named period. Returns total_sales, net_sales, average_sales, total_orders, total_items, total_tax, total_shipping, total_refunds, total_discount.

period.description: week | month | last_month | year. Mutually exclusive with date_min/date_max.

tools.name: woocommerce_top_sellers_report
tools.description: Top-selling products for a date window or named period. Returns array of { title, product_id, quantity }.

tools.name: woocommerce_coupons_totals_report
tools.description: Coupon counts grouped by discount type (percent | fixed_cart | fixed_product). NOTE: this endpoint accepts NO query params — it returns store-wide totals, not a usage-over-time window. For per-coupon usage history pair this with `woocommerce_list_orders` filtered to a date window and aggregate over `coupon_lines` client-side.

tools.name: woocommerce_orders_totals_report
tools.description: Counts of orders grouped by status (pending, processing, completed, ...). Lightweight KPI snapshot of pipeline state.

tools.name: woocommerce_update_customer
tools.description: Partial-update a customer by id. Only fields you pass are touched. Use for fixing email, billing/shipping addresses, or password resets.

username.description: WC may reject username changes after creation depending on store settings.

tools.name: woocommerce_delete_customer
tools.description: Permanently delete a customer. WC does NOT trash customers — deletion is always permanent. `force=true` is mandatory. Optionally pass `reassign` to transfer the deleted customer's orders to another user id.

force.description: Must be true. WC REST refuses customer delete without it.

reassign.description: User id to reassign orders/content to. Omit to keep the data orphaned.

tools.name: woocommerce_create_customer
tools.description: Create a customer account. Email + at least one identifying field required. WC will hash the password and email a welcome message (unless suppressed by the store config).

username.description: Leave empty to let WC derive from email.

password.description: Initial password. Omit to let WC auto-generate and email.

billing.description: { first_name, last_name, company, address_1, address_2, city, state, postcode, country, email, phone }.

shipping.description: { first_name, last_name, company, address_1, address_2, city, state, postcode, country }.

tools.name: woocommerce_create_order
tools.description: Create an order. Useful for manual order creation, B2B quote-to-order flows, or migrating data from another system. Pass `line_items` as array of `{ product_id, quantity, variation_id?, total? }`. Defaults to `status=pending` — use `status=processing` to skip the pending step.

status.description: pending | processing | on-hold | completed. Default pending.

customer_id.description: Existing customer id, or 0 for guest order.

line_items.description: Array of { product_id, quantity, variation_id?, subtotal?, total? }.

set_paid.description: If true, mark order as paid immediately (sets `date_paid`).

tools.name: woocommerce_delete_order
tools.description: Move an order to trash, or pass `force=true` to delete permanently. Permanent delete is irreversible and unlinks all order notes / refunds.

tools.name: woocommerce_list_order_notes
tools.description: List notes attached to an order (both internal admin notes and customer-visible notes). Used for audit trails and to read the message history attached to an order.

tools.name: woocommerce_get_order_note
tools.description: Retrieve a single order note by id.

tools.name: woocommerce_delete_order_note
tools.description: Delete an order note. Always permanent — order notes do not support trash.

force.description: Must be true for WC to allow deletion of an order note.

tools.name: woocommerce_list_coupons
tools.description: List discount coupons in the store. Filter by code or product.

tools.name: woocommerce_create_coupon
tools.description: Create a discount coupon. Use for marketing campaigns, recovery emails, or B2B negotiated discounts.

amount.description: Decimal as string. For percent: a number like '10' = 10%.

individual_use.description: If true, can't be combined with other coupons.

usage_limit.description: Max total usages across all customers.

limit_usage_to_x_items.description: Max number of items the discount applies to.

tools.name: woocommerce_get_coupon
tools.description: Get a single coupon by id, including full usage stats (`usage_count`, `used_by` array).

tools.name: woocommerce_update_coupon
tools.description: Partial-update a coupon. Common use: extend expiry, change amount, raise usage limit, restrict to additional products.

code.description: WC allows code rename but it's risky for existing campaigns.

tools.name: woocommerce_delete_coupon
tools.description: Move a coupon to trash, or pass `force=true` to delete permanently.

tools.name: woocommerce_list_order_status_options
tools.description: Reference card: valid order status slugs. Use when calling `woocommerce_list_orders` (filter), `woocommerce_create_order` / `woocommerce_update_order_status`.

endpointMapping.staticResponse:
# WooCommerce order status — valid slugs

| Slug         | Description                                                                                |
|--------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `pending`    | Received, awaiting payment. Default for new orders that didn't reach payment.              |
| `processing` | Payment received, stock reduced, awaiting fulfilment.                                      |
| `on-hold`    | Awaiting payment confirmation (manual gateway like bank transfer / cheque). Stock reserved.|
| `completed`  | Fulfilled. Final state.                                                                    |
| `cancelled`  | Cancelled by admin or auto-expired. Stock returned.                                        |
| `refunded`   | Fully refunded. Stock returned.                                                            |
| `failed`     | Payment failed or was declined. Doesn't reduce stock.                                      |
| `trash`      | Soft-deleted. Set via `woocommerce_delete_order`, not via `update_order_status`.           |

## Filter syntax

In `woocommerce_list_orders`, comma-separated: `status=processing,on-hold`.

## Lifecycle gotchas

- `processing → completed` is typical fulfilment flow.
- `pending → cancelled` releases stock and is the right move for abandoned checkout cleanup.
- Plugins may register additional statuses (e.g. `wc-awaiting-shipment`); use `woocommerce_orders_totals_report` to see what your store actually has.

tools.name: woocommerce_list_product_type_options
tools.description: Reference card: valid `type` values for `woocommerce_create_product` / `woocommerce_update_product` and product type filter on search.

endpointMapping.staticResponse:
# WooCommerce product type — valid values

| Type        | Description                                                                                   |
|-------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `simple`    | Default. One SKU, one price, optional stock.                                                  |
| `grouped`   | Container for child products that customer buys together. No price on the parent.             |
| `external`  | Listed in catalogue but "Buy" button links off-site (affiliate / external).                   |
| `variable`  | Has variations (size / colour / etc.). Each variation is its own SKU + price + stock.         |

## When using `variable`

1. Create the parent with `type=variable` + `attributes` array describing the variation axes.
2. Create each variation via `woocommerce_update_product_variation` (the actual variations endpoint is `/products/{id}/variations` — create variations one by one or use `woocommerce_batch_update_products` on the parent).

## When using `grouped`

1. Create the children first (`simple` products with their own SKUs).
2. Create the parent with `type=grouped` + `grouped_products: [<child_ids>]`.

## Plugin types

Subscriptions, bookings, and similar plugins register additional types (`subscription`, `variable-subscription`, `booking`). Behaviour depends on the plugin — read its docs.

tools.name: woocommerce_list_stock_status_options
tools.description: Reference card: valid `stock_status` values for product create/update + list filter.

endpointMapping.staticResponse:
# WooCommerce stock status — valid values

| Value          | Description                                                                                 |
|----------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `instock`      | Available now.                                                                              |
| `outofstock`   | Not available. Add-to-cart hidden by default.                                               |
| `onbackorder`  | Not currently in stock but accepting orders (with delayed shipment).                        |

## Related: the `backorders` field

When `manage_stock=true` and `stock_quantity=0`, the `backorders` field controls behaviour:

| `backorders` | Behaviour                                                                                  |
|--------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `no` (default) | Show out-of-stock, block purchase.                                                       |
| `notify`     | Allow purchase, notify customer that backorder.                                            |
| `yes`        | Allow purchase silently as backorder.                                                      |

## In `woocommerce_search_products`

Filter with `stock_status=instock` (single value, not CSV). For multi-status filtering you'd need multiple calls.

tools.name: woocommerce_list_report_period_options
tools.description: Reference card: valid `period` values for `woocommerce_sales_report` and `woocommerce_top_sellers_report`. Plus the alternative date-range syntax.

endpointMapping.staticResponse:
# WooCommerce report period — valid values

For `woocommerce_sales_report` and `woocommerce_top_sellers_report`.

## Named periods (shortcut)

| Period       | Window                                                |
|--------------|-------------------------------------------------------|
| `week`       | Current week (Sunday or Monday start per WP setting). |
| `month`      | Current calendar month, day 1 → today.                |
| `last_month` | Entire previous calendar month.                       |
| `year`       | Current calendar year, Jan 1 → today.                 |

Usage: `period="month"`.

## Custom date range

Mutually exclusive with `period`. Use both `date_min` and `date_max`:

```
woocommerce_sales_report(date_min="2026-01-01", date_max="2026-03-31")
```

Dates are `YYYY-MM-DD` (inclusive). Interpreted in the store's timezone (`wordpress_get_settings.timezone_string` on the WP side).

## Endpoints that do NOT accept date filters

- `woocommerce_orders_totals_report` (counts by status — always store-wide).
- `woocommerce_coupons_totals_report` (counts by discount type — always store-wide).

For period-over-period growth, call `sales_report` twice and compute deltas in context.

tools.name: woocommerce_skill_inventory_optimization
tools.description: Skill recipe: find out-of-stock and low-stock products, cross-reference with demand (top sellers), and bulk-bump stock via batch update.

endpointMapping.staticResponse:
# Skill: inventory optimisation

Goal: identify stock gaps in the catalogue and bump quantities based on demand signals.

## Steps

1. **Out-of-stock products**
   - `woocommerce_find_low_stock` with `stock_status=outofstock&per_page=100`. Iterate pages until exhausted.

2. **Backorder products**
   - `woocommerce_find_low_stock` with `stock_status=onbackorder&per_page=100`.

3. **Demand leaders**
   - `woocommerce_top_sellers_report` with `period=month` (or `last_month`). Returns `[{ title, product_id, quantity }]`.

4. **Cross-reference** (agent-side)
   - OUT-OF-STOCK ∩ TOP-SELLERS = URGENT restock. Add note for buyer review.
   - OUT-OF-STOCK − TOP-SELLERS = low priority. Consider `catalog_visibility=hidden` if 3+ months stagnant.
   - LOW STOCK ∩ TOP-SELLERS = preemptive bump (call `woocommerce_get_product` to read current `stock_quantity`, then decide a target).

5. **Bulk-bump**
   - `woocommerce_batch_update_products` with `update=[ { id, stock_quantity, stock_status: 'instock' }, ... ]`. Up to 100 entries per call.
   - For items that should accept backorders: also `backorders: "notify"`. Run `woocommerce_list_stock_status_options` if you forget the values.

6. **Verify**
   - Repeat step 1: out-of-stock list should be smaller.

## Gotchas

- For ≥3 products, ALWAYS use `batch_update_products`. Don't fan out single calls.
- `top_sellers_report` returns top 5 by default — for deeper analysis, also fetch `sales_report` and pair with category breakdown.
- The decision logic (target quantity, backorder policy) is the agent's — the connector just persists.

tools.name: woocommerce_skill_product_seo_optimization
tools.description: Skill recipe: optimise a product's name, description, slug, and Yoast/Rank Math SEO meta.

endpointMapping.staticResponse:
# Skill: product SEO optimisation

Goal: improve a product's organic search performance.

## Steps

1. **Read the full product**
   - `woocommerce_get_product` with `productId=<id>`. Note `name`, `slug`, `short_description`, `description`, existing `meta_data`.

2. **Rewrite in context** (agent-side)
   - `name`: lead with the primary keyword. Brand/model precise. ≤ 60 chars.
   - `short_description`: ~150 chars, conversion-focused.
   - `description`: long-form HTML. Include keyword 2-3x naturally, plus secondary terms. Bullet key features.
   - `slug`: kebab-case, ≤ 60 chars, no stop words. Leave existing slug alone unless it's truly bad (changes break URLs).

3. **Persist core fields**
   - `woocommerce_update_product` with `productId=<id>` + the rewritten fields.

4. **Set SEO meta**
   - Call `wordpress_list_seo_meta_keys` if you don't remember the keys.
   - Append/overwrite entries in `meta_data`:
     ```json
     {
       "productId": <id>,
       "meta_data": [
         { "key": "_yoast_wpseo_metadesc", "value": "..." },
         { "key": "_yoast_wpseo_focuskw", "value": "..." }
       ]
     }
     ```
   - Send via `woocommerce_update_product`. `meta_data` is upsert: existing keys overwritten, new keys added, untouched keys preserved.

5. **For batch (≥5 products)**
   - Build an `update` array → `woocommerce_batch_update_products`.

## Gotchas

- Don't change `slug` on an indexed product. URL changes need a 301 upstream.
- For variable products: optimise the parent + each variation separately (variation has its own description / sku / price).
- Verify that Yoast/Rank Math is installed and at a version that registers `meta_data` keys in REST — see `wordpress_list_seo_meta_keys`.

tools.name: woocommerce_skill_upsell_cross_sell
tools.description: Skill recipe: build upsell + cross-sell relationships for a product based on category, price tier, and tags.

endpointMapping.staticResponse:
# Skill: upsell / cross-sell recommendations

Goal: configure the 'You may also like' relationships rendered by WooCommerce.

## Steps

1. **Read the target product**
   - `woocommerce_get_product` with `productId=<id>`. Note `categories`, `tags`, `regular_price`, current `upsell_ids` / `cross_sell_ids`.

2. **Find candidates**
   - Same category, similar price: `woocommerce_search_products` with `category=<id>&min_price=<X>&max_price=<Y>&stock_status=instock&per_page=20`.
   - Same tag: `woocommerce_search_products` with `tag=<tag_id>&stock_status=instock&per_page=20`.

3. **Score candidates** (agent-side)
   - **UPSELL** = higher tier: 1.3-2.0× the source price, same category.
   - **CROSS-SELL** = same tier: 0.7-1.3× the source price, complementary (different functional category).

4. **Pick 3-5 per list** based on margin, current stock, and complementarity. Reject anything `stock_status != instock`.

5. **Persist**
   - `woocommerce_update_product` with:
     ```json
     {
       "productId": <id>,
       "upsell_ids": [<3-5 higher-tier ids>],
       "cross_sell_ids": [<3-5 same-tier ids>]
     }
     ```

## Where they appear

- **Upsells**: product page, 'You might also like' block.
- **Cross-sells**: cart page, 'Frequently bought with' block.

## Gotchas

- Both fields are ARRAYS OF PRODUCT IDS — not objects.
- Don't include the source product itself (filter `id !== productId`).
- Stagnant relationships: re-run periodically (e.g. monthly) as the catalogue evolves.

tools.name: woocommerce_skill_low_conversion_analysis
tools.description: Skill recipe: identify products visible in the catalogue but not selling, with an honest framing of what WooCommerce REST can and cannot measure.

endpointMapping.staticResponse:
# Skill: low-conversion product analysis

Goal: identify products that exist in the catalogue but underperform.

## Honest framing

WooCommerce REST does NOT expose impressions, add-to-cart, or page-view data — only purchases. So 'low conversion' here is actually 'low purchases per catalog-day'. For real funnel metrics, pair with a web analytics connector (Google Analytics, Plausible).

## Steps

1. **List visible candidates**
   - `woocommerce_search_products` with `status=publish&stock_status=instock&per_page=100`. Page through.

2. **List top sellers for the window**
   - `woocommerce_top_sellers_report` with `period=month` (or `last_month`). Returns `[{ product_id, quantity }]`.

3. **Set difference** (agent-side)
   - `visible_ids − top_seller_ids` = products in catalogue with zero/few sales in the window.

4. **Filter by age** — exclude products that haven't had time to sell
   - `woocommerce_search_products` with `before=<14_days_ago_iso>` to keep only products older than 14 days.

5. **Diagnose each candidate** (agent-side)
   - `woocommerce_get_product` on each. Common low-conversion causes:
     - Missing/bad images.
     - Short / generic `description`.
     - Price out-of-line with category siblings.
     - Missing SEO `meta_data`.
     - No `upsell_ids` / `cross_sell_ids`.

6. **Generate recommendations**
   - For each candidate, propose specific fixes. Then run the corresponding skill: `woocommerce_skill_product_seo_optimization`, `woocommerce_skill_upsell_cross_sell`, or surface for manual buyer review.

## Gotchas

- 'Zero sales' over a short window can be noise. Use month or last_month, not week, for confidence.
- For seasonal items, compare to same period last year if possible (custom `date_min` / `date_max`).
- Don't auto-hide products. Surface the list for human triage first.

tools.name: woocommerce_skill_abandoned_order_triage
tools.description: Skill recipe: process stuck orders (pending / on-hold / failed) — chase or cancel based on age and reason.

endpointMapping.staticResponse:
# Skill: abandoned-order triage

Goal: process orders that are stuck in pending / on-hold / failed and decide chase vs cancel.

## Honest framing

WC REST has NO 'abandoned cart' endpoint — that requires a plugin (e.g. WooCommerce Cart Abandonment Recovery). The closest proxy in core is `status=pending` (reached checkout, didn't complete payment).

## Steps

1. **List stuck orders**
   - `woocommerce_list_orders` with `status=pending,on-hold,failed&per_page=50&orderby=date&order=asc`. Oldest first.

2. **Per order** — `woocommerce_get_order` to see customer, line_items, payment_method, date_created.

3. **Classify by age + status** (agent-side)
   - `pending` > 24h → payment never completed. Email reminder OR cancel.
   - `on-hold` > 7d (manual gateway like bank transfer) → chase customer OR cancel.
   - `failed` → log + cancel (rarely worth chasing).

4. **Act per case**
   - Customer-visible reminder: `woocommerce_add_order_note` with `customer_note=true`. Triggers an email.
   - Internal audit note: `woocommerce_add_order_note` with `customer_note=false`.
   - Give up on the order: `woocommerce_update_order_status` with `status=cancelled`. Releases stock automatically.
   - For permanent cleanup: `woocommerce_delete_order` with `force=true`.

## Gotchas

- Cancelling restores stock to inventory immediately.
- Some gateways (Stripe, PayPal) auto-expire pending after 24-48h — check before manual cleanup.
- Customer emails go through WP's mailer; ensure SMTP is configured.
- For a real abandoned-cart funnel (started checkout but didn't finish), install the cart-abandonment-recovery plugin and add its endpoints in a v2.

tools.name: woocommerce_skill_revenue_kpi_snapshot
tools.description: Skill recipe: build a one-page KPI snapshot — revenue, AOV, top sellers, pipeline state, coupon mix.

endpointMapping.staticResponse:
# Skill: revenue / KPI snapshot

Goal: produce a one-page markdown KPI report for a date window.

## Steps

1. **Sales totals for the window**
   - `woocommerce_sales_report` with `period=month` (or `date_min` / `date_max` for custom). Returns `total_sales`, `net_sales`, `average_sales`, `total_orders`, `total_items`, `total_tax`, `total_shipping`, `total_refunds`, `total_discount`.

2. **Top sellers for the same window**
   - `woocommerce_top_sellers_report` with the same period / dates.

3. **Store-wide pipeline + coupon mix** (always store-wide, no date filter)
   - `woocommerce_orders_totals_report` → counts grouped by status.
   - `woocommerce_coupons_totals_report` → counts grouped by discount type.

4. **(Optional) Period-over-period**
   - Call `sales_report` again with the previous window (same length, ending right before this window's start). Compute deltas in context.

5. **Compose the markdown report** (agent-side)
   - Headline: window, total revenue, orders, AOV (= net_sales / total_orders).
   - Top sellers table.
   - Pipeline state (status counts as a small table).
   - Coupon mix (counts per discount type).
   - Deltas vs previous period if available.

## Gotchas

- Currency comes from `woocommerce_currency` site option (read via `wordpress_get_settings`).
- Reports use store timezone, not UTC — same gotcha as scheduled publishing.
- `*_totals_report` endpoints don't accept date filters — they're snapshots, not windowed. Don't try to backdate them.
- `top_sellers_report` returns top 5 by default. To rank deeper, the agent must combine multiple `sales_report` calls or query `list_orders` with date filter.
